title: Untargeted Top-down Proteomics by LC-MS/MS on Lumos
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"81g4d","text":"Describes the LC-MS/MS data acquisition procedure for top-down proteomics samples using the Thermo Scientific Orbitrap Fusion Lumos Tribrid mass spectrometer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8qt0o","text":"PLRP-S 5-μm particles 1,000-Å pore size (Agilent Technologies)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8vubl","text":"Water Optima LC/MS Grade (Fisher Scientific #W64)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"di7hr","text":"Acetonitrile Optima LC/MS Grade (Fisher Scientific #A955-4)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9dk4k","text":"Formic Acid LC/MS Grade (Thermo Scientific #28905)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"49gc2","text":"15 μm SilicaTip PicoTip Emitter (New Object #FS360-50-15-N-20-C12)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"df93o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4mbnm","text":"Buffer A: 94.8 % water, 5 % acetonitrile, 0.2 % formic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fq7eu","text":"Buffer B: 4.8 % water, 95 % acetonitrile, 0.2 % formic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Samples were analyzed on a Thermo Scientific Orbitrap Fusion Lumos Tribrid mass spectrometer in line with a Dionex Ultimate 3000 RSLCnano system

Samples ( 6 µL ) were injected via the autosampler and loaded onto a self-packed trap column (150 μm i.d. x 2 cm length packed with PLRP-S 5-μm particles 1,000-Å pore size) for 00:10:00 with 100% loading buffer (94.8% water:5% acetonitrile:0.2% formic acid)

Following a valve switch and initiation of the nanopump at 300 nL/min (buffer A: 94.8 % water, 5 % acetonitrile, 0.2 % formic acid; buffer B: 4.8 % water, 95 % acetonitrile, 0.2 % formic acid), proteins were separated on a self-packed analytical column (75 μm i.d. x 25 cm length packed with PLRP-S 5-μm particles 1,000-Å pore size) according to the following gradient for fractions 1-4: %s %s %BValve position 0 51_1010 51_2121562556795729575 590 5 For fraction 5 and later, nanopump used the following gradient: %s %s

Eluted proteins were ionized in positive ion mode nanoelectrospray ionization (nESI) using a pulled tip nanospray emitter (15-μm i.d. ×125 mm) packed with 1mm of PLRP-S 5-μm particles 1,000-Å pore size with a custom nano-source ( https://proteomicsresource.washington.edu/docs/protocols05/UWPR_NSI_Source.pdf ). A B 1 High-High 2 Spray voltage 1800 3 Sweep gas 0 4 Ion transfer tube temp 320 5 Application mode Intact Protein 6 Pressure mode Low Pressure 7 Advanced Peak Determination True 8 Default charge state 15 9 S-lens RF 30 10 Source fragmentation 15 eV Global MS parameters

Precursor (intact protein) spectra were acquired at 120k FTRP A B 1 High-High 2 Detector type Orbitrap 3 Resolving power 120000 4 m/z RP measured 200 m/z 5 Scan range 600-2000 6 Mass range Normal 7 AGC target 1000000 8 Normalized AGC target 250% 9 Max Injection Time 100 ms 10 Microscans 4 11 Data type Profile 12 Polarity Positive 13 Use wide quad isolation True Parameters for MS1 acquisition

The mass spectrometer was operated using a Top2 data-dependent acquisition mode. Precursor ions were filtered by intensity, charge state, and dynamic exclusion. A B 1 Intensity minimum 20000 2 Intensity maximum 1E20 3 Included charge states 6-60 4 Include undetermined charge states False 5 Dynamic exclusion after n times 1 6 Dynamic exclusion duration 60 s 7 Mass tolerance 1.5 m/z 8 Exclude isotopes True Precursor selection filters for DDA

Ions for fragmentations were isolated and fragmented via higher energy dissociation (HCD) A B 1 High-High 2 Detector type Orbitrap 3 Isolation mode Quadrupole 4 Resolving power 60000 5 m/z RP measured 200 m/z 6 Scan range 350-2000 7 AGC target 1000000 8 Normalized AGC target 2000% 9 Max injection time 400 ms 10 Microscans 4 11 Isolation window 3 m/z 12 Activation type HCD 13 Collision energy 27 14 Collision energy mode Fixed 15 Polarity Positive Parameters for MS2 acquisition
